2. **Turn Around**: Turn your head or body around to face the direction you came from, towards the starting point. If you're facing the correct direction, you should see the road you just came from.

3. **Walk Back**: Walk back along the same path you took to reach the junction. Use the road markings, signs, or any other landmarks you passed earlier to guide you back.

4. **Continue Walking**: Keep walking until you reach the starting point. Pay attention to your surroundings to ensure you're walking in the right direction and that you haven't missed any turns.

5. **Verify Your Arrival**: Once you reach the starting point, you can verify that you've arrived by checking the location or by looking for any specific features that you know are at the starting point.
